evidence.scientific_clarity: Two-panel ROC+PRC clearly shows all 4 classifiers; diagonal and prevalence baselines present; AUROC/AP annotated in legends as required by brief.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set anywhere in gen_figure.py; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap-capable) DejaVu Sans fonts — not editable Type 1/42; DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default.
evidence.palette: Wong (2011) 8-color palette used explicitly; colors are colorblind-safe; no red/green directional abuse; four distinct hues plus linestyle redundancy.
evidence.layout_density: constrained_layout=True, figsize 7.2x3.3 inches; both panels fill space well; legends placed lower-right/upper-right; tick marks inward; no gaping whitespace visible.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and references legend annotations; legend inside panels (no overlap with curves); 'Chance' label in PRC legend is slightly redundant with caption text, minor quibble only.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists but pdffonts shows Type 3 fonts (DejaVu Sans), not selectable/editable Type 42; no pdf.fonttype=42 in script — text is rasterized in PDF.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present (figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, gen_figure.py); all non-trivial in size.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption provides bold title, explains both panels, identifies reference lines, gives specific AUROC and AP values for all 4 methods, and states data provenance.

overall_strengths: Scientifically complete: both panels, correct reference lines (diagonal ROC, prevalence PRC), and per-method AUROC/AP annotations satisfy the brief exactly.
Colorblind-safe Wong palette with linestyle redundancy, clean constrained layout, and a detailed caption with numerical findings make this near reviewer-ready.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype=0 settings — fonts embed as Type 3 DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default), so text is not selectable/editable in the vector PDF.
DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default font; a journal-preferred sans-serif (Helvetica, Arial, or a serif such as Times) would improve typographic polish.
